title: Update CLAUDE.md and README.md for new repo structure
description:
Rewrite CLAUDE.md and README.md to reflect the symlink-based structure, removing Ansible references and documenting the new workflow.

Steps:
- Step 1: Update CLAUDE.md 'What This Is' section — remove Ansible description, describe symlink approach
- Step 2: Update CLAUDE.md 'Commands' section — replace ansible commands with make install, make diff, make brew-sync, make brew-dump
- Step 3: Update CLAUDE.md 'Architecture' section — document dotfiles/, hosts/, install.sh, Makefile structure
- Step 4: Remove or replace Ansible-specific tags and uv/mise setup instructions in CLAUDE.md
- Step 5: Update (or create) README.md with: repo purpose, prerequisites, make install usage, Brewfile drift workflow, per-machine override instructions

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: CLAUDE.md contains no references to ansible/, uv, mise, or Ansible commands
- AC2: CLAUDE.md 'Commands' section accurately describes make targets and quick zsh copy
- AC3: README.md explains the symlink approach and how to set up a new machine
- AC4: Architecture section documents dotfiles/, hosts/<machine>/, install.sh, and Makefile

title: Remove vestigial directories: ansible/, system/, apps.md
description:
Delete the Ansible-based infrastructure and stale files that the symlink approach replaces.

Steps:
- Step 1: Delete ansible/ directory entirely
- Step 2: Delete system/ directory entirely
- Step 3: Delete apps.md (references stale tools like Atom and YADR)
- Step 4: Update .gitignore if it references removed paths
- Step 5: Verify keyboardio/ is untouched

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: ansible/ directory no longer exists in the repo
- AC2: system/ directory no longer exists in the repo
- AC3: apps.md no longer exists in the repo
- AC4: keyboardio/ is unchanged
- AC5: No dangling references to removed paths in remaining files

title: Create Makefile with install and brew targets
description:
Write a Makefile at repo root with five targets covering install, diff, and Brewfile drift management.

Steps:
- Step 1: Add 'make install' target → runs ./install.sh
- Step 2: Add 'make diff' target → runs git diff (live files are repo files)
- Step 3: Add 'make brew-install' target → runs brew bundle install --global
- Step 4: Add 'make brew-sync' target → dumps to /tmp/.Brewfile.current, diffs vs dotfiles/.Brewfile (read-only drift view)
- Step 5: Add 'make brew-dump' target → runs brew bundle dump --force (writes live state to ~/.Brewfile → repo file)

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: make install invokes ./install.sh
- AC2: make diff runs git diff
- AC3: make brew-install runs brew bundle install --global
- AC4: make brew-sync shows bidirectional drift between live brew state and dotfiles/.Brewfile without modifying anything
- AC5: make brew-dump writes live brew state to ~/.Brewfile (which is the symlinked repo file)

title: Create install.sh symlink installer
description:
Write install.sh at repo root. Detects machine via hostname -s, symlinks individual files (not directories), backs up conflicts, applies shared dotfiles then host-specific overrides.

Steps:
- Step 1: Detect machine name via hostname -s; check if hosts/<machine>/ exists
- Step 2: For each file under dotfiles/, compute target path (strip dotfiles/ prefix, prepend ~/); apply symlink logic
- Step 3: Symlink logic per file: (a) target absent → create parent dirs + symlink; (b) already symlink into this repo → skip; (c) existing file/foreign symlink → rename to <target>.bak.<timestamp> then symlink
- Step 4: After shared files, process hosts/<machine>/ with same logic, overriding shared files of same name
- Step 5: Script must be idempotent (safe to run multiple times)

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: Running install.sh on mbp2022 creates symlinks for all files under dotfiles/ into ~/
- AC2: Running install.sh twice produces no errors and skips already-correct symlinks
- AC3: A pre-existing ~/file gets renamed to ~/file.bak.<timestamp> before the symlink is created
- AC4: Script does NOT install software, run brew bundle, or touch anything outside ~/
- AC5: If hosts/<machine>/ does not exist, only shared dotfiles are installed (no error)

title: Migrate dotfiles from ansible/ to dotfiles/ directory
description:
Move all shared config files from ansible/roles/workstations/files/ into a new dotfiles/ directory mirroring ~/. Also create hosts/mbp2022/ placeholder and dotfiles/.config/ghostty/ placeholder.

Steps:
- Step 1: Create dotfiles/ directory structure (dotfiles/.zshrc, dotfiles/.zsh/, dotfiles/.zsh.before/, dotfiles/.zsh.after/, dotfiles/.vimrc, dotfiles/.Brewfile, dotfiles/.config/ghostty/)
- Step 2: Move zsh/.zshrc → dotfiles/.zshrc
- Step 3: Move zsh/.zsh/*.zsh → dotfiles/.zsh/*.zsh
- Step 4: Move zsh/.zsh.before/ → dotfiles/.zsh.before/
- Step 5: Move zsh/.zsh.after/ → dotfiles/.zsh.after/
- Step 6: Move vim/.vimrc → dotfiles/.vimrc
- Step 7: Move homebrew/.Brewfile-mac → dotfiles/.Brewfile
- Step 8: Create hosts/mbp2022/ empty placeholder directory
- Step 9: Create dotfiles/.config/ghostty/ placeholder
- Step 10: Verify dotfiles/.zsh/secrets.zsh sources from ~/.secrets (never from the repo) — credentials must never be committed

Acceptance criteria:
- AC1: All files from ansible/roles/workstations/files/ exist at their new paths under dotfiles/
- AC2: hosts/mbp2022/ directory exists (can be empty)
- AC3: dotfiles/.config/ghostty/ placeholder exists
- AC4: No files in the old ansible/ paths are missing from dotfiles/
- AC5: secrets.zsh (if present) sources credentials from ~/.secrets, not from any path inside the repo

title: Import ai-config repo contents into bootstrap repo
description: Merge or incorporate the contents of the ai-config repository into this bootstrap repo so AI tooling configuration is managed in a single place.

title: Import Terminal configuration into bootstrap
description: Add terminal emulator configuration files to the bootstrap repo so terminal settings are managed as dotfiles and deployed via install.sh. Determine which terminal(s) are in use (Terminal.app, iTerm2, Ghostty, Warp, etc.) and locate their config files for inclusion under dotfiles/ or hosts/mbp2022/.
close_reason: Ghostty config added to dotfiles/.config/ghostty/config; macOS Terminal skipped (binary plist, low priority); symlinking deferred to bs-vab drift audit

title: Check and update system hostnames
description: Audit current hostnames for all managed systems (mbp2022, lemp9) and ensure they are correctly configured. Verify that hostname -s returns the expected values since install.sh uses this for per-machine override detection.
close_reason: Renamed machine hostname to mbp2022 via scutil; now matches hosts/mbp2022/ and hostname -s output

title: Dotfiles redesign: symlink-based config management
description: Eliminate config drift by making repo files the live files via symlinks. Editing ~/.zshrc becomes editing the repo file — no sync step needed. Replaces the Ansible-based approach with a lightweight install.sh + Makefile workflow. Covers zsh, vim, Homebrew Brewfile, and app configs (ghostty). Per-machine overrides supported under hosts/<machine>/.

title: Rename master to main and update GitHub
description: Rename the default branch from 'master' to 'main' in both the local repo and on GitHub. Update any references to 'master' in config files, CI, or documentation.
close_reason: Local branch renamed master->main, upstream tracking updated to origin/main, CLAUDE.md updated

title: Configure tmux dotfiles
description:
Iterative setup of tmux config in dotfiles, worked through together with user to get preferences right.

Scope: add/update dotfiles/.tmux.conf (or equivalent) with user-preferred settings.

Known items to address:
- Change prefix key from ctrl+b to ctrl+space
- Add tmux-specific zsh aliases (e.g. attach, new session shortcuts)

Additional settings to discover during session (keybindings, status bar, copy mode, splits, etc).

title: Audit and update .gitignore for post-ansible repo
description: The repo previously used Ansible but has since migrated away from it. The .gitignore likely contains Ansible-specific entries (e.g. ansible/.venv, *.retry, inventory secrets) that are no longer relevant. Audit the current .gitignore, remove stale Ansible-era entries, and ensure current tooling (mise, uv, install.sh) is properly covered.

title: Audit current machine config drift against repo
description: Check current machine's live settings (Homebrew packages, Zsh config, dotfiles, tool versions) against what's defined in the repo. Identify any drift — packages installed but not in Brewfile, configs in ~ not symlinked from repo, or repo files not deployed. Goal: catch manual changes made outside of the install/Ansible workflow before they're lost.
close_reason: Audited all live config against repo: Brewfile updated from live state, added mise.zsh, fixed 0_path.zsh, brought in .gitconfig, tmux.conf, and ssh config to hosts/mbp2022. Remaining drift is unlinked files — resolved by running install.sh.
